assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Acceptance Criteria row shows 'PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. The Acceptance Criteria Detail table lists all 5 criteria (single-license filter, comma-separated multi-license, 400 for invalid, pagination integration, unchanged response shape) each with Result=PASS. Separate criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) each contain 'Verdict: PASS' with detailed reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Scope Containment row states: 'PASS | PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files (`list.rs`, `service/mod.rs`) and 1 created file (`tests/api/package.rs`) align with Files to Modify and Files to Create'.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Review Feedback row shows 'N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. The Root-Cause Investigation row shows 'N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). These are verification/analysis documents. No code files were created or modified. The report contains only read-only analysis of the diff with code excerpts shown as quotes/evidence, not modifications.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents all findings in a summary table format. There is no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action. The report presents findings for human review with detailed evidence tables.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Sensitive Patterns row shows 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines across all 3 files'. The Security Scan Detail section elaborates: 'No sensitive patterns detected in the PR diff. The added code handles user input (license query parameter) with proper validation via the `spdx` crate before using it in database queries, preventing injection of arbitrary values.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Test Change Classification row shows 'ADDITIVE | All test files are newly created; 4 new test functions with 80 lines of test coverage added'.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md summary table contains all expected verdicts: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (N/A) from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns (PASS) from Security; CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A) from Correctness; Test Quality (PASS) and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE) from Style/Conventions. The Test Quality Detail section confirms it combines Repetitive Test Detection (PASS), Test Documentation (PASS), and Eval Quality (N/A).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: Scope Containment details '2 modified files (`list.rs`, `service/mod.rs`) and 1 created file (`tests/api/package.rs`)' with file-by-file comparison. Security: Security Scan Detail section provides line-level analysis: 'No sensitive patterns detected in the PR diff. The added code handles user input (license query parameter) with proper validation via the `spdx` crate'. Correctness: Acceptance Criteria Detail table has 5 rows with per-criterion code-level evidence (e.g., criterion 1 cites 'validate_license_param parses single value; is_in filter with InnerJoin'). Style/Conventions: Test Quality Detail section assesses repetitive test detection ('4 test functions have distinct setups'), test documentation ('All 4 test functions have /// doc comments'), and eval quality. Additionally, 5 separate criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) provide multi-paragraph code-level evidence for each acceptance criterion.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Test Quality row states 'Eval Quality: N/A'. The Test Quality Detail section confirms: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- No eval result reviews found on this PR.' Test Quality overall is PASS, combining only Repetitive Test Detection (PASS) and Test Documentation (PASS), with Eval Quality not affecting the result.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A as shown in report.md Test Quality Detail. No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the outputs. The Root-Cause Investigation row confirms 'N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, Review Feedback shows 'N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and Root-Cause Investigation shows 'N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These appear as the first two rows in the summary table, before domain-specific verdicts, indicating they are determined independently by the orchestrator. Their verdicts (N/A) are based on orchestrator-level conditions (no reviews, no sub-tasks) rather than domain analysis results.
